flashcards.back: A fundamental building block of a computer system that performs a specific, well-defined task. Examples include the ALU, Control Unit, and Registers within the CPU.

flashcards.front: What is the role of the Arithmetic and Logic Unit (ALU)?
flashcards.back: The ALU is the computational heart of the CPU. It performs all arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction) and logical operations (AND, OR, NOT, XOR) on data.

flashcards.front: Describe the purpose of the Control Unit (CU).
flashcards.back: The Control Unit acts as the manager of the CPU. It fetches instructions from memory, decodes them, and generates control signals to coordinate all other functional units to execute the instruction.

flashcards.front: What are Registers and why are they important?
flashcards.back: Registers are small, high-speed storage locations located inside the CPU. They temporarily hold data, instructions, and memory addresses that are currently being processed, allowing for extremely fast access.

flashcards.front: What are the main functional units in a standard von Neumann architecture computer?
flashcards.back: The main functional units are: 1) Central Processing Unit (CPU) containing the ALU, Control Unit, and Registers, 2) Memory Unit, and 3) Input/Output (I/O) subsystems.
